mission_description: Artemis 2 Orbit Animation - NASA's first crewed Artemis lunar mission
mission_keywords: artemis, artemis2, artemis ii, orion, integrity, moon, nasa, sls, space, astronomy, animation

ui.pageTitle: NASA's Artemis 2 Mission 2026 - An Orbit Animation

missionStart.label: Launch
missionStart.infoText: Launch: 1st Apr 2026, 22:35:12 UTC - SLS launches Artemis II from Kennedy Space Center LC-39B
missionStart.hoverText: Launch from LC-39B. Orion's public trajectory is not yet published at this point.

solarArrays.label: Solar Arrays
solarArrays.infoText: 1st Apr 2026, 22:44 UTC - Orion deploys solar arrays in Earth orbit
solarArrays.hoverText: Service module arrays deploy in Earth orbit before published free-flight ephemeris begins.

perigeeRaise1.label: Perigee Raise
perigeeRaise1.infoText: 1st Apr 2026, 23:13 UTC - Perigee raise maneuver targeting a 2223 x 185 km Earth orbit
perigeeRaise1.hoverText: ICPS raises Orion's perigee during phasing; public Orion ephemeris is not yet available.

apogeeRaise.label: Apogee Raise
apogeeRaise.infoText: 2nd Apr 2026, 00:11:57 UTC - Apogee raise maneuver targeting 70,377 km
apogeeRaise.hoverText: ICPS stretches Orion's orbit to high apogee before the published free-flight track begins.

icpsSeparation.label: ICPS Sep
icpsSeparation.infoText: 2nd Apr 2026, 01:59:30 UTC - Orion separates from the ICPS during the early translunar mission phase
icpsSeparation.hoverText: Nominal Orion/ICPS separation milestone from the Apr 4 HORIZONS major-events table.

dataStart.label: Data Start
dataStart.infoText: 2nd Apr 2026, 01:58:33 UTC - First Orion ephemeris sample used by this app's selected data window
dataStart.hoverText: First sampled Orion ephemeris point for this mission view. Event chronology can include milestones before and after the sampled orbit window.

upperStageSeparationBurn.label: Upper Stage Burn
upperStageSeparationBurn.infoText: 2nd Apr 2026, 03:16 UTC - Orion upper stage separation burn
upperStageSeparationBurn.hoverText: Orion performs a separation burn to open distance from the spent upper stage.

icpsDisposalPacific.label: ICPS Disposal
icpsDisposalPacific.infoText: 2nd Apr 2026, 03:24 UTC - ICPS disposal burn for Pacific splashdown
icpsDisposalPacific.hoverText: ICPS commits to its nominal disposal path toward a remote Pacific splashdown corridor.

cubesatDeploy.label: CubeSats Deploy
cubesatDeploy.infoText: 2nd Apr 2026, 03:28 UTC - International partner CubeSats begin deploying at one-minute intervals
cubesatDeploy.hoverText: International CubeSats are released into deep-space trajectories at one-minute intervals.

perigeeRaise2.label: Perigee Raise 2
perigeeRaise2.infoText: 2nd Apr 2026, 11:30:12 UTC - Perigee raise burn late on flight day 1
perigeeRaise2.hoverText: Orion completes the final Earth-orbit shaping burn ahead of translunar injection.

tli.label: TLI
tli.infoText: 2nd Apr 2026, 23:49:12 UTC - Trans-lunar injection burn starts; Apr 4 HORIZONS lists roughly 5m55s duration
tli.hoverText: The major translunar burn sends Orion onto a lunar free-return trajectory.

tliEnd.label: TLI End
tliEnd.infoText: 2nd Apr 2026, 23:54:12 UTC - End of translunar injection burn (delta-v approximately 388 m/s)
tliEnd.hoverText: Marks the end of Orion's translunar injection burn in the Apr 4 HORIZONS major-events table.

earthShadowEntry.label: Earth Shadow In
earthShadowEntry.infoText: 3rd Apr 2026, 00:10:12 UTC - Orion enters Earth shadow
earthShadowEntry.hoverText: Earth shadow entrance during outbound translunar coast.

earthShadowExit.label: Earth Shadow Out
earthShadowExit.infoText: 3rd Apr 2026, 01:16:12 UTC - Orion exits Earth shadow
earthShadowExit.hoverText: Earth shadow exit before the longer outbound coast to lunar flyby.

trajectoryCorrection1.label: OTC-1 CXL
trajectoryCorrection1.infoText: 3rd Apr 2026, 22:43:12 UTC - [Cancelled] Orbit trajectory correction burn #1 in the Apr 4 HORIZONS update
trajectoryCorrection1.hoverText: HORIZONS major-events table marks this outbound correction as cancelled.

trajectoryCorrection2.label: OTC-2 CXL
trajectoryCorrection2.infoText: 4th Apr 2026, 23:43:12 UTC - [Cancelled] Orbit trajectory correction burn #2 in the Apr 4 HORIZONS update
trajectoryCorrection2.hoverText: HORIZONS major-events table marks this outbound correction as cancelled.

trajectoryCorrection3.label: OTC-3
trajectoryCorrection3.infoText: 6th Apr 2026, 03:03:17 UTC - Orbit trajectory correction burn #3 starts (18s burn, delta-v about 3 m/s)
trajectoryCorrection3.hoverText: Apr 6 HORIZONS update provides explicit OTC-3 begin/end timing; this event duration reflects that split row.

lunarSoiEntry.label: Lunar SOI In
lunarSoiEntry.infoText: 6th Apr 2026, 05:38:44 UTC - Orion enters the Moon's sphere of influence (Hill sphere definition, around 62,800 km from the Moon)
lunarSoiEntry.hoverText: Updated Apr 6 HORIZONS timing for lunar SOI entry using the Hill-sphere definition.

earthSet.label: Earthset
earthSet.infoText: 6th Apr 2026, 22:45 UTC - Earth sets behind the lunar limb from Orion's flyby perspective
earthSet.hoverText: NASA public flyby timeline marks Earthset at 6:45 p.m. Eastern.

closestApproach.label: Lunar Flyby
closestApproach.infoText: 6th Apr 2026, 23:01:12 UTC - Closest approach to the Moon during Artemis II's crewed lunar flyby (about 8,282.5 km)
closestApproach.hoverText: Updated Apr 6 HORIZONS timing and distance for lunar closest approach during the crewed free-return flyby.

maxDistanceEarth.label: Max Distance
maxDistanceEarth.infoText: 6th Apr 2026, 23:05:12 UTC - Orion reaches maximum distance from Earth (about 413,146.2 km)
maxDistanceEarth.hoverText: Updated Apr 6 HORIZONS timing and range for Orion's farthest distance from Earth.

earthRise.label: Earthrise
earthRise.infoText: 6th Apr 2026, 23:25 UTC - Earth rises over the lunar horizon from Orion's flyby perspective
earthRise.hoverText: NASA public flyby timeline marks Earthrise at 7:25 p.m. Eastern.

eclipseStart.label: Eclipse In
eclipseStart.infoText: 7th Apr 2026, 00:35 UTC - Orion enters solar eclipse during outbound coast
eclipseStart.hoverText: NASA public flyby timeline marks solar-eclipse entry at 8:35 p.m. Eastern.

eclipseEnd.label: Eclipse Out
eclipseEnd.infoText: 7th Apr 2026, 01:32 UTC - Orion exits solar eclipse
eclipseEnd.hoverText: NASA public flyby timeline marks solar-eclipse exit at 9:32 p.m. Eastern.

lunarSoiExit.label: Lunar SOI Out
lunarSoiExit.infoText: 7th Apr 2026, 17:27:12 UTC - Orion exits the Moon's sphere of influence
lunarSoiExit.hoverText: Orion departs the Moon's sphere of influence and begins the homeward leg.

returnCorrection1.label: RTC-1
returnCorrection1.infoText: 8th Apr 2026, 00:04:12 UTC - Return trajectory correction burn #1
returnCorrection1.hoverText: First return correction fine-tunes Orion's Earth re-entry corridor.

manualPiloting.label: Manual Demo
manualPiloting.infoText: 9th Apr 2026, 02:55:12 UTC - Crew manual piloting demonstration during the return leg
manualPiloting.hoverText: Crew evaluates manual handling and displays during a piloting demonstration on return.

returnCorrection2.label: RTC-2
returnCorrection2.infoText: 10th Apr 2026, 03:04:12 UTC - Return trajectory correction burn #2
returnCorrection2.hoverText: Second return correction refines targeting before entry preparations.

returnCorrection3.label: RTC-3
returnCorrection3.infoText: 10th Apr 2026, 19:04:12 UTC - Return trajectory correction burn #3
returnCorrection3.hoverText: Final return correction lines Orion up for service module separation and entry.

serviceModuleSeparation.label: CSM Separation
serviceModuleSeparation.infoText: 10th Apr 2026, 23:44:12 UTC - Orion crew and service module separation
serviceModuleSeparation.hoverText: The crew module separates from the service module before atmospheric entry.

crewModuleRaiseBurn.label: CM Raise Burn
crewModuleRaiseBurn.infoText: 10th Apr 2026, 23:47:12 UTC - Crew module raise burn before atmospheric entry
crewModuleRaiseBurn.hoverText: A small burn positions the crew module for the proper entry attitude.

entryInterface.label: Entry Interface
entryInterface.infoText: 11th Apr 2026, 00:04:12 UTC - Entry interface at 122 km above Earth. Later descent milestones listed in HORIZONS include forward bay cover deployment at 11 km, drogue parachute deployment at 7.6 km, and pilot parachute deployment at 2.9 km, but HORIZONS does not publish absolute timestamps for those sub-events.
entryInterface.hoverText: Orion reaches the 122 km entry interface before its parachute sequence milestones.

splashdown.label: Splashdown
splashdown.infoText: 11th Apr 2026, 00:17:12 UTC - Pacific splashdown near Baja California after the nominal 10-day Artemis II mission
splashdown.hoverText: The crew module splashes down in the Pacific near Baja California.

finalPowerDown.label: Power Down
finalPowerDown.infoText: 11th Apr 2026, 00:32:12 UTC - Orion final power down
finalPowerDown.hoverText: Orion safes its systems after splashdown and recovery operations begin.
